claim: Context is becoming as important as code in agent systems; context should have its own engineering discipline with generation, evaluation, distribution, and observability.
actionable: Treat agent prompts, instructions, and skills as code artifacts requiring versioning, testing, review, and observability; implement a Context Development Lifecycle.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Reusable prompts and instructions should be standardized using formats like agent.md or similar to enable portability across agent frameworks.
actionable: Maintain a standardized agent.md file with your reusable instructions, making it easy to port prompts between frameworks and share with team members.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Validation evals check if context follows the required format and schema; grammarly-style evals assess if agents can understand the context; rule-based evals verify compliance with team conventions.
actionable: Write evals at three levels: schema validation (is the format correct?), comprehension checks (can the agent understand this?), and rule verification (does generated code follow conventions?).
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: End-to-end evals execute agent-generated code in a sandbox to verify it works, catching errors that static analysis misses.
actionable: After validating generated code syntax, deploy it to a sandbox and test it live to ensure the agent's output actually works and follows your conventions.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Context evals are non-deterministic; measure success as a percentage of passes over multiple runs, and set error budgets rather than expecting 100% pass rates.
actionable: When evaluating agent context, run evals 5+ times and track success rate; set error budgets for critical evals and accept variability for others.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Skills are reusable context packages that can contain instructions, scripts, and documents, enabling distribution as a standard format across teams and organizations.
actionable: Package reusable agent capabilities as skills (instructions + helpers), version them, and distribute via a registry or package manager for reuse across projects.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Dependency management applies to context packages; version conflicts and compatibility issues require careful tracking, similar to library versioning.
actionable: Track context package versions and dependencies; test compatibility when combining multiple skills to avoid context conflicts.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Agent logs provide feedback on whether context is being used; if many agents report missing information, that signals the need for new context to be distributed to the team.
actionable: Monitor agent logs for repeated failures or missing knowledge; when a pattern emerges, create context (skill/instructions) to address it and distribute to the team.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: PR review feedback is a signal to improve context; instead of arguing on the PR, use the feedback to update agent instructions so the next iteration improves automatically.
actionable: When reviewing PRs generated by agents, capture feedback and update your agent instructions rather than manually fixing each PR; this scales improvements.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Production runtime failures of agent-generated code should trigger test case creation to prevent recurrence, treating code failures as context feedback.
actionable: When agent-generated code fails in production, create an eval or test case based on that failure, then improve context so the agent learns to avoid it.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: A context flywheel emerges when better context leads to better agent output, which creates better observations, which improves context iteratively.
actionable: Set up feedback loops so that improved context leads to better agent output, which generates insights that further refine context; this creates compounding improvement.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl

claim: Team-scale context practices require creating team norms around when to add context if something is missing, scaling improvements beyond individual developers.
actionable: Establish a team reflex: whenever an agent is missing knowledge, add context (skill/instruction) instead of doing the work manually; this scales improvements across the team.
source_title: Context Is the New Code — Patrick Debois, Tessl
